name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
فَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ ٱلْغُرُوبِ (٣٩)
```

```english
So be patient at what they say, and celebrate the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before the sunset, (39)
```

```arabic
وَمِنَ ٱلَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ وَأَدْبَـٰرَ ٱلسُّجُودِ (٤٠)
```

```english
and glorify Him through part of the night and after the prostrations. (40)
```

```arabic
وَٱسْتَمِعْ يَوْمَ يُنَادِ ٱلْمُنَادِ مِن مَّكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ (٤١)
```

```english
And be on the alert for the day when the caller calls from a close quarter, (41)
```

```arabic
يَوْمَ يَسْمَعُونَ ٱلصَّيْحَةَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ ٱلْخُرُوجِ (٤٢)
```

```english
the day when they hear the Cry in all truth. That is the day of rising [from the dead]. (42)
```

```arabic
إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْىِۦ وَنُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْنَا ٱلْمَصِيرُ (٤٣)
```

```english
Indeed it is We who give life and bring death, and toward Us is the [final] destination. (43)
```

```arabic
يَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلْأَرْضُ عَنْهُمْ سِرَاعًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ حَشْرٌ عَلَيْنَا يَسِيرٌ (٤٤)
```

```english
The day the earth is split open for [disentombing] them, [they will come out] hastening. That mustering is easy for Us [to carry out]. (44)
```

```arabic
نَّحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِجَبَّارٍ ۖ فَذَكِّرْ بِٱلْقُرْءَانِ مَن يَخَافُ وَعِيدِ (٤٥)
```

```english
We know well what they say, and you are not there to compel them. So admonish by the Quran those who fear My threat. (45)
```

## (Commentary)

The conclusion of the *surah* commands the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله to be patient with what they say—their accusations of sorcery, madness, and poetry, and their stubborn mockery regarding the Resurrection (المعاد) — *the return of all beings to Allah for judgment* — and the return to Allah, the Exalted. He صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله is commanded to remain patient, to worship his Lord by glorifying Him, to anticipate the Resurrection by waiting for the Blast, and to remind through the Quran those who fear Allah in the Unseen (الغيب) — *that which lies beyond sensory perception and empirical knowledge*.

His saying, the Exalted: **فَاصْبِرْ عَلى ما يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ** "So be patient with what they say, and celebrate the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before sunset" (50:39). This is a derivation from all that preceded regarding the polytheists' denial of the Resurrection, the detailed discourse on the Resurrection and the proofs for it, and the warning to those who deny it and treat the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله as a liar, threatening them with the same fate that befell the deniers of past nations.

His saying: **وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ** "and celebrate the praise of your Lord" etc., is a command to declare His transcendence (تنزيهه) from what they say, accompanied by praise (الحمد). Its sum meaning is the affirmation of beautiful action for Him and the negation of every deficiency and blemish from Him, the Exalted. The glorification before the rising of the sun is applicable to the Dawn prayer (صلاة الصبح), and the glorification before sunset is applicable to the Afternoon prayer (صلاة العصر), or to both the Noon (الظهر) and Afternoon prayers.

His saying, the Exalted: **وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ وَأَدْبارَ السُّجُودِ** "And during the night, celebrate His glory, and after the prostrations" (50:40). That is, during the night, glorify Him therein; this is applicable to the Sunset (المغرب) and Night (العشاء) prayers.

Regarding His saying: **وَأَدْبارَ السُّجُودِ** "and after the prostrations," the word **الأدبار** (the backs/ends) is the plural of *dubur* (دبر), which refers to the end of a thing and what follows it. What is meant by "after the prostrations" is after the prayers, as prostration is the final part of a unit of prayer (الركعة); thus, it applies to the post-prayer supplications and litanies (التعقيب). It has also been said that it refers to the supererogatory prayers (النوافل) performed after the obligatory ones (الفرائض), or specifically the two or more units of prayer after the Sunset prayer, or the *Witr* (الوتر) prayer at the end of the night.

His saying, the Exalted: **وَاسْتَمِعْ يَوْمَ يُنادِ الْمُنادِ مِنْ مَكانٍ قَرِيبٍ** "And listen for the day when the caller calls out from a nearby place" (50:41). The exegetes have interpreted "listening" (الاستماع) in various ways, but the most likely is that it implies the meaning of "waiting" (الانتظار), with the phrase "the day when the caller calls out" being its object. The meaning is: Wait for a day in which the caller calls out, lending your ear to hear his call. The "call of the caller" refers to the blowing of the Trumpet by its master, as indicated by the following verse.

The call being from a "nearby place" (مكان قريب) is due to its encompassing them; it reaches their hearing in an equal proportion, not differing by proximity or distance, for it is the call of the Resurrection and the word of life.

His saying, the Exalted: **يَوْمَ يَسْمَعُونَ الصَّيْحَةَ بِالْحَقِّ ذلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخُرُوجِ** "the day when they will hear the Cry in all truth: that is the Day of Coming Forth" (50:42). This is an explanation of the day the caller calls. The Cry (الصيحة) being "in all truth" (بالحق) is because it is a decreed and inevitable judgment, as previously mentioned in His saying: **وَجاءَتْ سَكْرَةُ الْمَوْتِ بِالْحَقِّ** "The agony of death brings the truth" (50:19).

His saying: **ذلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخُرُوجِ** "that is the Day of Coming Forth," meaning the day of coming forth from the graves, as the Exalted said: **يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ الْأَجْداثِ سِراعاً** "the day when they will emerge from the graves, hastening" (70:43).

His saying, the Exalted: **إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْيِي وَنُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْنَا الْمَصِيرُ** "It is We who give life and make to die, and toward Us is the return" (50:43). What is meant by giving life (الإحياء) is the bestowing of life upon dead bodies in this world, and by making to die (الإماتة) is the causing of death in this world, which is the transition to the realm of the grave (عالم القبر). By His saying: **وَإِلَيْنَا الْمَصِيرُ** "and toward Us is the return," He refers to the giving of life through the Resurrection in the Hereafter, as indicated by the context.

His saying, the Exalted: **يَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ الْأَرْضُ عَنْهُمْ سِراعاً ذلِكَ حَشْرٌ عَلَيْنا يَسِيرٌ** "The day when the earth splits open for them [to emerge] hastening: that is a gathering easy for Us" (50:44). The origin of **تَشَقَّقُ** is *tatashaqqaqu*, meaning the earth cracks open for them, and they emerge from it rushing toward the Caller.

His saying: **ذلِكَ حَشْرٌ عَلَيْنا يَسِيرٌ** "that is a gathering easy for Us," meaning that what We have mentioned regarding their emergence from the graves that split open for them as they hasten is a gathering (حشر) — *the assembling of all creation for judgment* — that is easy for Us.

His saying, the Exalted: **نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَقُولُونَ وَما أَنْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ بِجَبَّارٍ فَذَكِّرْ بِالْقُرْآنِ مَنْ يَخافُ وَعِيدِ** "We know best what they say, and you are not one to compel them. So remind by the Quran him who fears My threat" (50:45). This serves as the rationale (التعليل) for His saying: **فَاصْبِرْ عَلى ما يَقُولُونَ** "So be patient with what they say." The "compeller" (الجبار) is the one with absolute authority who forces people to do what he wants.

The meaning is: Be patient with what they say, glorify the praise of your Lord, and wait for the Resurrection; for We know best what they say and We shall soon requite them for what they have done. You are not a dominant compeller over them such that you could force them into the faith in Allah and the Last Day to which you invite them. Since this is their state, then remind through the Quran those who fear My threat.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, it is narrated by al-Tabarani in *al-Awsat* and Ibn Asakir from Jarir ibn Abdillah from the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله regarding His saying: **وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ** — "Before the rising of the sun is the Dawn prayer, and before sunset is the Afternoon prayer."

In *al-Majma'*, it is narrated from Abu Abdillah عليه‌السلام that he was asked about His saying: **وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ** and he said: **تقول حين تصبح وحين تمسي عشر مرات : لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له ـ له الملك وله الحمد وهو على كل شيء قدير** — "You should say ten times when you reach the morning and when you reach the evening: 'There is no god but Allah alone, without partner; to Him belongs the kingdom and to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things.'"

I say: This is derived from the general application of "glorification" (التسبيح) in the verse, even if its specific instance refers to the Dawn and Afternoon prayers; there is no contradiction.

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from Hariz from Zurarah from Abu Ja'far عليه‌السلام, who said: "I asked about **وَأَدْبارَ السُّجُودِ** (and after the prostrations)." He replied: "The units of prayer after the Sunset prayer."

I say: al-Qummi also narrated it in his *Tafsir* with his chain from Ibn Abi Nasr from Imam al-Rida عليه‌السلام, and the wording is: "Four units of prayer after the Sunset prayer."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Musaddad narrated in his *Musnad*, as did Ibn al-Mundhir and Ibn Mardawayh from Ali ibn Abi Talib, who said: "I asked the Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله about 'after the stars' (أدبار النجوم) and 'after the prostrations' (أدبار السجود). He said: 'After the prostrations are the two units of prayer after the Sunset prayer, and after the stars are the two units of prayer before the Morning prayer.'"

I say: Similar reports have been narrated from Ibn Abbas and Umar from him صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله, and it was also attributed in *Majma' al-Bayan* to al-Hasan ibn Ali عليه‌السلام from the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله.

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **فَذَكِّرْ بِالْقُرْآنِ مَنْ يَخافُ وَعِيدِ** he said: "Remind [them], O Muhammad, of the punishment We have promised."
